GEO Tools

Your customers ask AI before they ask Google, and most brands have no idea whether they show up or lose to a competitor. Every other tool hands you a visibility score and stops there. Scribble does the whole loop: audit where you're invisible across every AI engine, create content that closes the gap, and amplify it through 50,000 creators who only get paid when AI cites them. A gap report won't win customers. Being the cited answer will.

### Explore the tech stack and tools that power Scribble Network. See what products Scribble Network uses for development, design, marketing, analytics, and more.

Engineering & Development 1

OpenRouter Model Fusion

OpenRouter Model FusionRun many models side by side and fuse the best answer

5.0 (35 reviews)

Unified API

Also considered:

clorocloro

We use both. Cloro runs most of our models (GPT, Gemini, Perplexity, Copilot) and is the more economical option there. We added OpenRouter specifically for Grok, since cloro dropped Grok support, and tracking all five engines reliably is the whole point. Different tool per model.

Finance 1

Stripe

StripeFinancial infrastructure for the internet

4.9 (458 reviews)

Invoicing tools

• [...] •

Wireframing

•

Interface design tools

Honestly, Figma's been our #1 from the start, we never really shopped around. Real-time collaboration, everything in the browser, and the whole team (design, feedback, iteration) working in one file. It's just where our work lives.

results.title: Scribble Network: The product that makes AI recommend your brand
results.content:
So we built the thing nobody else has: the first AI Search tool married to a creator platform.

We don't just measure your AI visibility, we fix it.

Our product attributes each brand mention back to the exact creator post that earned it.

And the platform now pays creators when they get cited. Creators love this.

This is our edge. We came at this from distribution and not measurement.   
  
We never asked 'how do we track this?', we asked 'how do we actually get brands cited?'  
  
The answer to this already lives in Scribble; try it on your own brand and tell me what you find.

Honest feedback wanted.

My co-founders and I are here all day answering everything.

Mohsin Ali
PicWish

PicWish

@kaavya\_prasad interesting approach with the creator bounties! [...] Scribble Network

Scribble Network
kaavya prasad

5d ago

### Our thesis: creator content moves AI visibility. Here's the data from two clients.

Hello Fam, [...] kaavya prasad
Scribble Network

Scribble Network

@hazy0 We're going the smart contract route for this. We will write scribbler <> citation data on chain with the copy of the answer and the engine, with a timestamp on a public blockchain. This will trigger a USDC payment instantly as the conditions are met. All logs will be viewable on a block explorer - we'll obviously make it a bit more readable. And yes - this is useful for creators as it stands now. But over time this data graph builds, and we could potentially have an industry <> Creator citation probability that is verifiable. Brands might really like that. Thinking along these lines at the moment.

Hazy
